---
title: RPC events
description: Watch contract deployments, invocations, transfers, and RPC events over daemon WebSocket.
---

## Watching contract activity over RPC

Daemon WebSocket events let applications observe deployments, invocations, transfers, and `fire_rpc_event` payloads. Use a dedicated struct for the contract-side payload so off-chain clients receive named fields instead of a positional `any[]`.

[Open in Playground](https://playground.xelis.io/?%3D)

```rust filename="rpc_notice.slx" copy
struct RpcTransferNotice {
    caller: Address,
    amount: u64,
    asset: Hash,
    height: u64
}

entry notify_transfer(amount: u64, asset: Hash) -> u64 {
    fire_rpc_event(90, RpcTransferNotice {
        caller: Transaction::current().unwrap().source(),
        amount,
        asset,
        height: Block::current().height()
    })
    return 0
}
```

Subscribe once per event name:

```js copy
const ws = new WebSocket('ws://127.0.0.1:8080/json_rpc')

ws.send(JSON.stringify({
  id: 1,
  jsonrpc: '2.0',
  method: 'subscribe',
  params: {
    notify: 'contract_event'
  }
}))

ws.onmessage = (message) => {
  console.log(message.data)
}
```

Useful event names for contract development:

- `contract_deploy`: a contract was deployed.
- `contract_invoke`: a contract entry was invoked.
- `contract_event`: a contract called `fire_rpc_event`.
- `contract_transfers`: a contract transferred assets.
- `new_asset`: a new asset was created.
